pages.full_text:
the mould grows & grows into the shape of a tho light (shade). or whatever. To kill the mould you heat it when you get it out of the mould. It's water & fire resistant. (A company in the US is using huge warehouses to make packaging materials of Ikea).
Another man had made an iphone speaker out of bamboo. Magnifying the sound without electricity.
Another student with shakey hands had a hot water boiling water dispenser so old people didn't have to lift a kettle.
    We drove on & found a carpark next to St. Mary's Headley & Box Hill, a well-kept church, for lunch.
    On to see Arathna & Godfrey. Eng 12. & Evans 10, spent all our visit playing with Tinder.
pages.notes: Page continues from previous spread mid-sentence. 'Arathna' may be a name rather than a place; 'Eng 12' and 'Evans 10' appear to be ages or reference numbers. A small sketch of the bamboo iphone speaker appears in the middle of the page. 'tho light' may read 'the light' or 'tho' as abbreviation. 'Tinder' appears to be a pet (dog).

pages.full_text:
We are at a farm above Steyning. We walked 1½ miles to the Chequers where we had good ham & chips but no pudding.
    Tinder has been very good.
Friday
    We visited Simon & Claudia Ogden for elevenses. They were very welcoming. Then we joined the Gravestocks who gave us a delicious lunch. We went for a walk through a park & watched water skiing & saw the sea.
    At △ we left for our campsite The Ship & Anchor at Littlehampton ***
There's a bike path to Arundel, but it's very overgrown.
photo_captions:
PRAYER OF ST RICHARD OF CHICHESTER
Thanks be to thee, my Lord Jesus Christ,
For all the benefits which Thou hast given me,
For all the pains and insults
Which Thou hast borne for me,
O most merciful Redeemer,
Friend and Brother,
May I know Thee more clearly,
Love Thee more dearly
And follow Thee more nearly.
CHICHESTER CATHEDRAL
pages.notes: Handwritten page number '88' in top-right corner; there appears to be a struck-through number above the text near top (possibly '87' crossed out, or an artefact). A glued-in printed card for 'Prayer of St Richard of Chichester' from Chichester Cathedral is pasted at the bottom of the page. 'At △' likely means 'at 4 [o'clock]' (triangle used as shorthand for the numeral 4). '***' after campsite name likely indicates a star rating. 'Chequers' is a pub/restaurant near Steyning. 'Gravestocks' is a family name, not a place.
